metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Duskmourn: House of Horror features balanced color and archetype distribution with average gameplay pace that varies by archetype. The format emphasizes 2-drops and synergistic interactions rather than early stomps.

color_notes.{W} White: Best color. Two excellent common removals (Trapped in the Screen, Seized from Slumber) and strong creatures (Fear of Surveillance, Unsettling Twins).
color_notes.{R} Red: Proactive color with good removal (Scorching Dragonfire, Glassworks // Shattered Yard). Creatures support aggressive strategies.
color_notes.{G} Green: Most flexible color. Essential for delirium decks and provides mana fixing for splashes.
color_notes.{U} Blue: Solid removal and control pieces (Glimmerburst, Unable to Scream) but creatures are mediocre.
color_notes.{B} Black: Weakest creatures; valuable primarily for removal (Murder, Winter's Intervention, Final Vengeance).

key_gameplay_notes: Format rewards 2-drops and synergies over big early plays.
Manifest information is deducible — what was binned reveals manifested card quality.
Splashing requires 3+ sources; green enables most splashes via mana fixing.
Rooms provide essential mana sinks that prevent mana flood.
Delirium enablers (Say Its Name, Commune with Evil) act as near-guarantees.
Eerie is easily triggered with high enchantment density.
Early 2-drop trades prevent late-game surprises from manifests.

mechanics.name: Rooms
mechanics.summary: Dual-faced enchantments providing Eerie triggers and mana sinks. Excellent flexibility — you unlock one half at a time.
mechanics.performance: Format-defining. Rooms are mana sinks that smooth long games.

mechanics.name: Eerie
mechanics.summary: Easy-to-trigger payoff whenever a Room or aura enters. WU version is stronger than black's.
mechanics.performance: WU Eerie is tier 1; BU lags behind.

mechanics.name: Survival
mechanics.summary: Disappointing mechanic; works better as a subtheme. Some Survivors are still worth playing on rate.
mechanics.performance: Weak primary theme; some standout Survivors exist.

mechanics.name: Delirium
mechanics.summary: Both RG and BG versions are solid. Enablers like Say Its Name and Commune with Evil nearly guarantee activation.
mechanics.performance: Reliable archetype payoff; build with enablers.

mechanics.name: Manifest Dread
mechanics.summary: Slows games down and is core to GU. Requires careful information deduction since both faces are hidden.
mechanics.performance: Engine for GU Manifest decks; rewards card-counting.

mechanics.name: Impending
mechanics.summary: Rare mechanic. All Overlords are bombs; Overlord of the Mistmoors is called "the most broken rare" in the set.

tier_1.name: WU Eerie
tier_1.strategy: Tempo / tempo-control with enchantment synergies. Optimistic Scavenger and Gremlin Tamer are the signposts; chain Rooms and auras for repeated Eerie triggers.

tier_1.name: RW Aggro
tier_1.strategy: Fast damage with tempo creatures. Ragged Playmate and Vicious Clown anchor the curve.

tier_2.name: RG Delirium
tier_2.strategy: Graveyard setup enabling delirium payoffs. Fear of Burning Alive and Spineseeker Centipede are key.

tier_2.name: GU Manifest
tier_2.strategy: Manifest dread creatures repeatedly and turn the top of the deck into card advantage. Growing Dread and Oblivious Bookworm are signposts.

tier_2.name: BR Sacrifice
tier_2.strategy: Fodder generation with sacrifice payoffs. Boilerbilges Ripper and Glassworks // Shattered Yard reward repeated sacrifice triggers.

tier_2.name: BG Delirium
tier_2.strategy: Graveyard synergies. Broodspinner is the payoff; Say Its Name turns delirium on near-automatically.

tier_3.name: GW Survival
tier_3.strategy: Trigger Survival from creature damage. Defiant Survivor and Glimmer Seeker are the signposts.

tier_3.name: UR Rooms
tier_3.strategy: Dual-faced Room activation with Rampaging Soulrager. Rewards drafting many Rooms.

tier_3.name: WB Reanimator
tier_3.strategy: Graveyard recursion archetype. Emerge from the Cocoon and Shroudstomper are the targets.

tier_3.name: UB Eerie / Control
tier_3.strategy: Control-focused Eerie deck. Nowhere to Run and Final Vengeance provide removal.

general_tips: Take 2-drops early — the format rewards establishing a board on turn 2.
Manifest information is deducible: which cards have left zones reveal what manifest faces hide.
Splashing requires 3+ sources; green is the splash enabler via fixing.
Rooms are the main mana sink — every deck wants at least a couple.
Delirium activates almost for free with Say Its Name or Commune with Evil.
High enchantment density makes Eerie trivially good — prioritize enchantments in WU/UB.
Trade 2-drops liberally to keep manifests from snowballing.
